event: Saint George's Day celebration in Bolnisi
symbols: feast table laden with bottles — communal celebration and Georgian hospitality traditions
man holding a banner or cloth — festive display or toast-making in Georgian supra tradition
dark background — Pirosmani's characteristic style evoking the night setting of celebrations
three figures — fellowship and communal gathering
description: A fragment of a naive-style painting showing three Georgian men around a long white table covered with dark bottles and plates. The figure at left holds up what appears to be a banner or large cloth. The two men at the table sit behind the feast. The figures have characteristic simplified, round faces typical of Pirosmani's folk style. The background is dark olive-green. Several dark objects, possibly shoes or loaves, lie on the ground beneath the table. The scene captures a moment of festive gathering in a deliberately flat, iconic compositional style.
